title: Office handoff: auto-review + extension GUI shipped & pushed; verify live harness dry-run, then decide apply
context: Session on the home machine built the VS Code extension GUI surface (v0.4.0) and the harness-driven `hunch auto-review`. All code is committed (95dcc2b) and PUSHED (origin/main == HEAD). Decisions dec_7a7f944536 (extension GUI), dec_cd37bf2d9a (auto-review) recorded. Moving to the office; this is the resume point.
decision: RESUME AT OFFICE. State on arrival: (1) `git pull` — everything is on origin/main already (0 ahead / 0 behind at handoff). (2) The VS Code extension is installed on the HOME machine at v0.4.0; at the office run `npm i` in vscode-extension/, `npm run build`, package with vsce and `code --install-extension` (or press F5) to get v0.4.0 there too. (3) `hunch auto-review` is DRY-RUN by default — the home-machine live harness dry-run (21 subscription calls, serial, slow) had not printed its plan before handoff; RE-RUN `npm run hunch -- auto-review` at the office to see the accept/delete/keep plan. (4) Only after eyeballing that plan, decide whether to `hunch auto-review --apply` (it DELETES duplicate + harness-irrelevant drafts and confirms verified+relevant ones — irreversible from the store). (5) The Critic gate is intentional (dec_a466655539): auto-accept requires the draft already be verified+grounded; the harness can only veto. If you want the harness to also approve UNVERIFIED drafts, that needs a new flag + a decision superseding dec_a466655539.
consequences: Nothing has been --applied yet: the 21 real drafts are untouched; the plan must be reviewed before any deletion.
Tests: 46 pass (incl. 7 planner tests). Typecheck clean except the pre-existing node:sqlite decl errors.
Extension v0.4.0 installed on home machine only; office needs a build+install.
